title: eschscholzia ramosa var. trichophylla
intro: Eschscholzia ramosa var. trichophylla, also known as the California poppy, is a wildflower native to the western coast of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties. The health benefits of Eschscholzia ramosa var. trichophylla include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as pain relief and improved digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation and alleviate various health issues.
